name: United Auto Credit Corporation
description_short: United Auto Credit provides non-prime auto financing to customers with challenged credit, serving 80,000+ customers for 28 years with flexible payment options and personalized service.
description_long:
United Auto Credit is an established non-prime auto lender that has been in business for 28 years, specializing in financing for consumers with less-than-perfect credit histories. The company positions itself as a relationship-focused lender rather than a purely transactional financial institution, emphasizing that customers are "people" deserving professional service, not just FICO scores. They currently serve over 80,000 customers and offer a range of auto financing solutions tailored to borrowers who may not qualify for traditional prime lending.

The company provides auto financing with flexible terms and multiple payment accommodation options. Customers can access their accounts through an online portal and mobile app to manage payments, set up automatic withdrawals on bi-weekly or monthly schedules, and track account progress. United Auto Credit offers customer support through live chat and a comprehensive FAQ section addressing common account management questions. Their payment options are designed for convenience, with the ability to change due dates once during a contract term (subject to account being current).

United Auto Credit distinguishes itself through its emphasis on customer service and flexibility during financial hardship. The company explicitly states they work with customers facing unexpected financial difficulties and position communication and early outreach as key to accessing multiple assistance options. They maintain no grace period policy but offer payment arrangement tools and emphasize proactive customer outreach to prevent delinquency.

As a non-prime auto lender, United Auto Credit serves a legitimate market need for consumers with challenged credit, but potential borrowers should understand that non-prime financing typically comes with higher interest rates and fees compared to traditional lending. The website does not disclose specific APR ranges, terms, or fee structures, which is common for lenders in this category but makes rate comparison difficult upfront.
address: 1805 Sardis Rd N STE 110, Charlotte, NC 28270

company_info.headquarters: 1805 Sardis Rd N STE 110, Charlotte, NC 28270

pros: 28 years of established business history in non-prime auto lending
Serves 80,000+ customers, indicating significant market presence and experience
Mobile app available for account management, payments, and progress tracking
Flexible payment options including automatic bi-weekly or monthly withdrawals
Ability to change payment due date once during contract term
Live chat support available for customer questions
Proactive customer assistance programs for those facing financial hardship
Online account enrollment and secure customer login portal
cons: No grace period for late payments; delinquency occurs one day past due with immediate finance charge accrual
Late charges assessed after certain days past due per state law or contract (specific thresholds not disclosed)
Website does not disclose specific APR ranges, loan terms, or fee structures, making comparison shopping difficult
Non-prime lender status typically indicates higher interest rates and less favorable terms than traditional auto financing
Limited information about loan approval criteria, down payment requirements, or vehicle eligibility on website
best_for: Consumers with subprime or challenged credit histories seeking auto financing
Borrowers who need flexible payment arrangements or assistance during financial hardship
Individuals who prefer relationship-based lending with direct customer support access
Customers managing existing United Auto Credit accounts who need account management tools
diagnosis: United Auto Credit is best for consumers with subprime credit who need auto financing and value personalized service and flexible payment accommodations. The primary caveat is that non-prime auto lending typically carries significantly higher interest rates and fees than traditional financing, and the company does not disclose specific rates or terms on their website, requiring potential borrowers to apply or contact the company directly for quotes.
services: Non-prime auto financing for customers with less-than-perfect credit
Secure online account portal with login and enrollment
Mobile app for account management and payment tracking
Automatic payment setup (bi-weekly or monthly withdrawal)
One-time payment due date change capability
Payment arrangement programs for customers facing financial difficulty
Customer assistance programs for delinquency prevention
